title: Chocolate Charts
description: Simulate an ever-growing recipe scoreboard to find the ten scores after N recipes, then find where the digit sequence of N first appears.
writeup:
## Approach

Two Elves maintain a scoreboard starting with `[3, 7]`. Each step they sum their current scores, append the decimal digits of the sum, then each Elf advances by `1 + their_score` positions (wrapping around).

## Part 1

Run the simulation until the scoreboard has at least `N + 10` entries, then read off `scores[N:N+10]`.

## Part 2

Search for the first occurrence of the digit sequence of `N` in the scoreboard. Each step appends 1 or 2 digits, so after each append we check both the last `n` and second-to-last `n` digits for a match.

Both parts share a single simulation loop that stops as soon as both answers are known.

## Code Highlights

- State is two integers (elf positions) plus a flat list of digits — no complex data structures needed.
- The dual-offset check (`scores[-n:]` and `scores[-n-1:-1]`) handles the 1-or-2-digit-append edge case cleanly.
